commit.message:
Restrict named capture group names to ASCII letters/digits (#130)

Match java.util.regex.Pattern rules: first character must be an ASCII
letter (A-Z, a-z), subsequent characters must be ASCII letters or
digits (A-Z, a-z, 0-9). Previously SafeRE was too lenient, accepting
underscores, Unicode letters/digits, and connector punctuation, and
also allowing names to start with digits.

Fixes #129

feat(nvim): add persistent folding (#15)

* Initial plan

* feat(nvim): add persistent folding

- Set viewoptions to explicitly include folds, cursor, curdir
- Add BufWinLeave autocmd to save view (mkview) for normal buffers
- Add BufWinEnter autocmd to restore view (loadview) for normal buffers
- Both use pcall for safe error handling and buftype guard to skip
  special buffers (help, quickfix, terminal, etc.)
- foldlevel and foldlevelstart remain at 99 (all folds open by default)

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/suasuasuasuasua/dotfiles/sessions/3995f218-1e3c-4d38-99d1-fe7e997f265e

Co-authored-by: suasuasuasuasua <72476123+suasuasuasuasua@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf(nvim): only load view once per buffer using view_activated flag

The previous implementation called loadview on every BufWinEnter,
causing repeated disk reads each time you switched between windows.
Now uses a per-buffer view_activated flag (AstroNvim-style) so:
- loadview runs only once per buffer, on first entry
- filetype check ensures treesitter has set up folding before restoring
- mkview is only called when the view has been activated, avoiding
  overwriting views for buffers that were never loaded
- BufWritePost and WinLeave added as additional save triggers

feat: chezmoi + mise migration foundation (Architecture & Design) (#42)

* feat: add chezmoi + mise migration foundation (issue #20)

Implements the architecture & design foundation for migrating from
Nix + Home Manager to chezmoi + mise. All files are additive —
existing Nix configuration is untouched (Phase 1: non-destructive).

New files:
- .chezmoi.toml.tmpl: chezmoi config template with machine/user detection
- .chezmoiignore: per-machine file exclusion with WSL/enterprise conditionals
- .chezmoiexternal.toml: external git repos (zsh plugins, tpm)
- .chezmoidata/packages.yaml: full tool audit (Tier 1–4 classification)
- .chezmoidata/users.yaml: user profiles for naroslife, enterpriseuser
- .mise.toml: global tool versions with ubi/pipx/cargo backends + task runner
- bootstrap.sh: new entry point (online/offline/no-mise modes)
- home/dot_zshrc.tmpl: Zsh config with kill-switch-aware aliases
- home/dot_gitconfig.tmpl: per-user git config via chezmoi template
- home/dot_ssh/config.tmpl: SSH config with WSL/enterprise conditionals
- home/dot_config/{starship,atuin,bottom,ripgrep}: tool configs
- home/run_onchange_install-zsh-plugins.sh.tmpl: plugin clone hook
- scripts/install-apt-deps.sh: Tier 1/3/4 apt dependency installer
- scripts/install-zsh-plugins.sh: git clone Zsh plugins + tpm
- scripts/install-fonts.sh: Nerd Fonts downloader
- scripts/kill-switch.sh: revert all modern CLI aliases (sourceable)
- docs/adr/ADR-001-chezmoi-mise-migration.md: architecture decision record

Updated:
- ARCHITECTURE.md: documents target + legacy architecture, migration status

Closes #20

commit.message:
fix(acp-log): suppress consecutive chunk frames via envelope.skip

Consecutive agent_message_chunk / agent_thought_chunk notifications
flood the ACP debug log. Only the first frame of each consecutive
same-kind + same-direction run is logged now.

Design:
- acpLogEnvelope gains updateKind (set by filterNotifySessionUpdateBody)
  and skip bool (set by the sink before deciding to log)
- acpLastRecord struct holds the last-recorded updateKind+dir pair
- defaultACPProcessLogSink is a pointer receiver with a mutex and a
  lastRecord field
- applyChunkFilter(dir, *envelope) is a focused helper: if the envelope
  is a chunk kind and matches lastRecord, it sets skip=true; otherwise
  it updates lastRecord (and resets it for non-chunk frames)
- Frame() builds the envelope, calls applyChunkFilter, returns early if
  envelope.skip is true
- filterACPBody / filterNotifySessionUpdateBody return ([]byte, string)
  to surface updateKind without a second JSON parse

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Add pipeExprToString() that flattens nested PipeExpr, tries single
  line first, wraps if > 100 chars
- Add flattenPipeChain() helper to collect stages from left-recursive
  PipeExpr AST
- Skip wrapping when any stage contains multi-line content (e.g.,
  function literals) to avoid indentation corruption
- Fix estimateEndLine() to account for multi-line pipe expressions,
  preventing spurious blank lines on re-format
- Add maxExprLine() helper for walking expression trees to find
  deepest source line

All 110 .kuki files pass idempotency testing. Reformatted 13 files
that benefit from the new wrapping.

fix: recharacterize ×5.0 as calibration mismatch, not bug, in glycogen report

The demand-diagnosis-glycogen report incorrectly called the ×5.0 multiplier
a 'time-integration bug' and 'double integration'. The companion report
(demand-calibration-fix-impact) confirmed:
  - ×5.0 is dimensionally correct (U/min × 5min = U/step)
  - Removing ×5.0 worsens R² (−0.47 → −0.71, EXP-1631)
  - The actual fix is patient-specific calibration β

Additional fixes:
  - Patient a actual dBG: −0.03 → +0.12 mg/dL/step (sign and magnitude)
  - '10/11 monotonic' → '10/11 increasing trend (7 strictly monotonic)'
  - 'β doubles' → 'β more than doubles (2.6×)' (0.273→0.715)
  - Updated stale line references (108→158, 544→546, 454→455)

fix: replace picotool-based Pico W detection with mount-based lsblk + cp flashing (#46)

* Initial plan

* fix: use mount-based detection and cp for Pico W flashing instead of picotool

Replace unreliable `picotool info -l` device detection with `lsblk`
mount-point detection for RPI-RP2 USB mass-storage devices. Flash via
`cp` to the mount point instead of `picotool load --bus --address`.

This fixes the BOOTSEL timeout on the Pi 4B CI runner where Linux
correctly mounts RPI-RP2 but picotool fails to enumerate the device
through its USB control endpoint.

Changes:
- detect_picos(): use lsblk LABEL/MOUNTPOINT with fallback to common
  mount directory scan instead of picotool info -l
- flash_one(): use cp + sync instead of picotool load/reboot
- force_bootsel(): gracefully skip when picotool is not installed
- picotool is now optional (only needed for headless BOOTSEL reboot)
- Increased settle times for USB re-enumeration and auto-mount
